parts.text:
Investigate prior-session provenance for this question without loading all transcripts into the parent session.

Question: Why does src/auth/queue.ts deduplicate refresh work per tenant instead of using one global mutex?
Expected relevant prior-session title: Auth refresh queue rationale
Ignore distractor sessions about billing retry and markdown parser.

You must use these tools in this exact workflow before answering:
1. session_lookup to find the relevant prior session by keyword/title.
2. session_detail with detail='messages' on the relevant blob/session to identify the exact message.
3. message_detail for one exact message before answering.

Return only a compact JSON object to me with:
{"answer":"...","evidence":{"session_id":"...","blob_id":"...","message_id":"..."},"why_not_global_mutex":"...","irrelevant_context_ignored":["..."]}

Use exact ids from the tools. If unavailable, use empty string.

parts.text: {"answer":"src/auth/queue.ts uses RefreshQueue with a per-tenant key so same-tenant duplicate refresh requests coalesce into one refresh while different tenants can continue in parallel.","evidence":{"session_id":"ses_1e5293edbffeNYKw5oAEIh0xR9","blob_id":"auth_queue","message_id":"msg_e1ad6c131001G12NezGzSzrckD"},"why_not_global_mutex":"A global mutex was rejected because auth_refresh_different_tenants_parallel showed unrelated tenants must not block each other.","irrelevant_context_ignored":["Billing retry queue rationale","Markdown parser onboarding cleanup"]}
